message:
fix(distributed): deduplicate recovery symbols by (SBN, ESI) instead of ESI alone

RecoveryCollector and StateDecoder previously deduped symbols using only
the ESI (encoding symbol identifier), which is unique within a single
source block but not across blocks. When recovering multi-block objects,
two symbols from different blocks could share the same ESI, causing the
second to be silently dropped as a "duplicate" and leaving the decoder
short of the required symbol count.

Both data structures now key on the tuple (SBN, ESI) — source block
number paired with encoding symbol identifier — which is globally unique
across all blocks of an object.

RecoveryCollector: esi_to_idx HashMap renamed to symbol_to_idx with key
type changed from u32 to (u8, u32). All documentation references to
"ESI deduplication" updated to "(SBN, ESI) deduplication".

StateDecoder: seen_esi HashSet renamed to seen_symbols with element type
changed from u32 to (u8, u32). max_block_size in the decoding config
now uses symbols_per_block * symbol_size instead of object_size, which
was incorrect for multi-block objects and would cause the decoder to
allocate oversized buffers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
